openmd by RufusLin: Preview of openmd-default.css styles.

dark: stygian-darkness outer-space deep-ocean burning-desert solar-flare midnight-blue emerald-foam lavender-mist saffron-sand

light: light-pineapple white-paper calm-sky boss-beige ancient-papyrus cream-paper formal-note

 
stygian-darkness  ·  dark

Stygian Darkness

A Secondary Heading

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for low-light ergonomic reading with a high-tech, futuristic character. The body typeface is IBM Plex Sans, a humanist sans-serif optimized for screen legibility. Headings use a technical display font (Orbitron) for h1 and h2 only.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Code Block

// Example: theme class applied to body element
const applyTheme = (name) => {
  document.body.className = name ? `theme-${name}` : '';
};
applyTheme('stygian-darkness');

Inline code spans and hyperlinks should feel integrated with the surrounding text rather than visually disruptive.

Reference Table

PropertyValueNotes
Theme namestygian-darknessCSS class applied to <body>
TypeDarkOptimized for low-light environments
Body fontIBM Plex SansUsed for all body text and h3–h6
Heading fontOrbitronApplied to h1 and h2 only
Code fontSource Code Pro / Roboto MonoUsed in pre and code blocks

Fourth-Level Heading

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

A blockquote excerpt, such as a definition or a cited passage, should be visually distinct but harmonious with the overall palette — set apart by indentation and a subtle left border rather than a jarring background color.

outer-space  ·  dark

Outer Space

A Secondary Heading

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for low-light ergonomic reading with a high-tech, futuristic character. The body typeface is IBM Plex Sans, a humanist sans-serif optimized for screen legibility. Headings use a technical display font (Share Tech Mono) for h1 and h2 only.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Code Block

// Example: theme class applied to body element
const applyTheme = (name) => {
  document.body.className = name ? `theme-${name}` : '';
};
applyTheme('outer-space');

Inline code spans and hyperlinks should feel integrated with the surrounding text rather than visually disruptive.

Reference Table

PropertyValueNotes
Theme nameouter-spaceCSS class applied to <body>
TypeDarkOptimized for low-light environments
Body fontIBM Plex SansUsed for all body text and h3–h6
Heading fontShare Tech MonoApplied to h1 and h2 only
Code fontSource Code Pro / Roboto MonoUsed in pre and code blocks

Fourth-Level Heading

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

A blockquote excerpt, such as a definition or a cited passage, should be visually distinct but harmonious with the overall palette — set apart by indentation and a subtle left border rather than a jarring background color.

The body typeface is IBM Plex Sans, a humanist sans-serif optimized for screen legibility. Headings use a technical display font (Orbitron) for h1 and h2 only.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

The body typeface is IBM Plex Sans, a humanist sans-serif optimized for screen legibility. Headings use a technical display font (Share Tech Mono) for h1 and h2 only.

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for low-light ergonomic reading with a high-tech, futuristic character. The body typeface is IBM Plex Sans, a humanist sans-serif optimized for screen legibility. Headings use a technical display font (Orbitron) for h1 and h2 only.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

PropertyValueNotes
Theme namesaffron-sandCSS class applied to <body>
TypeDarkOptimized for low-light environments
Body fontIBM Plex SansUsed for all body text and h3–h6
Heading fontOrbitronApplied to h1 and h2 only

Fourth-Level Heading

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

A blockquote excerpt, such as a definition or a cited passage, should be visually distinct but harmonious with the overall palette — set apart by indentation and a subtle left border rather than a jarring background color.

light-pineapple  ·  light

Light Pineapple

A Secondary Heading

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is Lora, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Code Block

// Example: theme class applied to body element
const applyTheme = (name) => {
  document.body.className = name ? `theme-${name}` : '';
};
applyTheme('light-pineapple');

Inline code spans and hyperlinks should feel integrated with the surrounding text rather than visually disruptive.

Reference Table

PropertyValueNotes
Theme namelight-pineappleCSS class applied to <body>
TypeLightOptimized for bright environments
Body fontLoraUsed for all body text and h3–h6
Heading fontLoraApplied to h1 and h2 only
Code fontSource Code Pro / Roboto MonoUsed in pre and code blocks

Fourth-Level Heading

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

A blockquote excerpt, such as a definition or a cited passage, should be visually distinct but harmonious with the overall palette — set apart by indentation and a subtle left border rather than a jarring background color.

white-paper  ·  light

White Paper

A Secondary Heading

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is Source Serif 4, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Code Block

// Example: theme class applied to body element
const applyTheme = (name) => {
  document.body.className = name ? `theme-${name}` : '';
};
applyTheme('white-paper');

Inline code spans and hyperlinks should feel integrated with the surrounding text rather than visually disruptive.

Reference Table

PropertyValueNotes
Theme namewhite-paperCSS class applied to <body>
TypeLightOptimized for bright environments
Body fontSource Serif 4Used for all body text and h3–h6
Heading fontSource Serif 4Applied to h1 and h2 only
Code fontSource Code Pro / Roboto MonoUsed in pre and code blocks

Fourth-Level Heading

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

A blockquote excerpt, such as a definition or a cited passage, should be visually distinct but harmonious with the overall palette — set apart by indentation and a subtle left border rather than a jarring background color.

calm-sky  ·  light

Calm Sky

A Secondary Heading

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is Libre Baskerville, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Code Block

// Example: theme class applied to body element
const applyTheme = (name) => {
  document.body.className = name ? `theme-${name}` : '';
};
applyTheme('calm-sky');

Inline code spans and hyperlinks should feel integrated with the surrounding text rather than visually disruptive.

Reference Table

PropertyValueNotes
Theme namecalm-skyCSS class applied to <body>
TypeLightOptimized for bright environments
Body fontLibre BaskervilleUsed for all body text and h3–h6
Heading fontLibre BaskervilleApplied to h1 and h2 only
Code fontSource Code Pro / Roboto MonoUsed in pre and code blocks

Fourth-Level Heading

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

A blockquote excerpt, such as a definition or a cited passage, should be visually distinct but harmonious with the overall palette — set apart by indentation and a subtle left border rather than a jarring background color.

boss-beige  ·  light

Boss Beige

A Secondary Heading

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is EB Garamond, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

Code Block

// Example: theme class applied to body element
const applyTheme = (name) => {
  document.body.className = name ? `theme-${name}` : '';
};
applyTheme('boss-beige');

Inline code spans and hyperlinks should feel integrated with the surrounding text rather than visually disruptive.

Lower-level headings inherit the body typeface to maintain readability in technical documents with deep heading hierarchies. A bold phrase or italicized term should be clearly distinguishable without disrupting the reading flow.

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is Crimson Pro, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is Spectral, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.

This theme uses a carefully chosen background and text color pairing designed for document-like readability, evoking printed paper in various tones. The body typeface is Source Serif 4, a classic serif chosen to evoke editorial and archival documents. Headings use the same serif as body text, maintaining a unified typographic voice.

Paragraph text is set at a comfortable line-height of 1.6. The color palette avoids both pure black backgrounds and pure white text, reducing eye strain during extended reading sessions. Accent colors — used for links and code highlights — are muted and desaturated, never fluorescent.
